neighborhoods.name: Summerville Historic District
neighborhoods.description: Charming downtown area with tree-lined streets, historic homes, Azalea Park, local shops and restaurants along Main Street
neighborhoods.character: Walkable downtown, Southern charm, historic architecture, small-town community feel

housing.predominantType: Older single-family homes, bungalows, and some historic Victorians

neighborhoods.character_notes: The heart of Summerville. Azalea Park and Hutchinson Square host community events. Saturday farmers market. Walkable to restaurants, shops, and Dorchester County Library. Older housing stock with charm but may need updates.

neighborhoods.name: Cane Bay Plantation
neighborhoods.description: Large master-planned community with new construction homes, Del Webb 55+ active adult section, resort-style amenities, and extensive trails
neighborhoods.character: Master-planned suburban, new construction, 55+ active adult community, resort amenities

housing.predominantType: New construction single-family homes, townhomes, Del Webb 55+ patio homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: One of SC's fastest-growing communities. Del Webb section is specifically designed for 55+ with clubhouse, pools, fitness center, pickleball, and organized activities. YMCA on-site. Miles of trails and lakes. Car required for everything outside the community.

neighborhoods.name: Nexton
neighborhoods.description: Modern mixed-use community with a walkable town center (Nexton Square), new construction homes, and a focus on connectivity and wellness
neighborhoods.character: Modern mixed-use, walkable town center, new urbanist design, tech-forward

housing.predominantType: New construction single-family homes, townhomes, and apartments near town center

neighborhoods.character_notes: Nexton Square town center has restaurants, shops, and a Publix. Brighton Park Village is the 55+ section by Del Webb. Community-wide gigabit fiber internet. Trails, swim club, and dog park. The most modern feel in the Summerville area. Premium pricing reflects newer builds.

neighborhoods.name: Ladson
neighborhoods.description: Unincorporated community north of Summerville along the I-26 corridor, more affordable housing, industrial and commercial areas nearby
neighborhoods.character: Suburban affordable, industrial corridor, working class, practical

housing.predominantType: Single-family ranch homes, manufactured homes, and older subdivisions

neighborhoods.character_notes: Most affordable option in the area. Close to I-26 for commuting. Exchange Park hosts the Coastal Carolina Fair. Walmart, Lowes, and commercial strip along US-78. Less charming than downtown Summerville but practical and budget-friendly. Some areas have flood risk.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Summerville Area

neighborhoods.name: Goose Creek
neighborhoods.description: City east of Summerville near Joint Base Charleston, affordable and diverse community with family-oriented neighborhoods

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes, townhomes, and military housing communities

neighborhoods.character_notes: Strong military community due to Joint Base Charleston proximity. More diverse than Summerville proper. Crowfield Plantation golf community is a popular option. Goose Creek Reservoir and municipal recreation programs. Very affordable housing market. Car essential with spread out suburban layout.
